key_insight: A reduced-basis hybrid TBNN-UDE framework can learn frame-invariant viscoelastic constitutive laws from shear data and still generalize to more complex flow regimes.
engineering_relevance: This enables more transferable digital-twin constitutive models that can be embedded directly into CFD solvers for predictive simulation of complex polymeric and viscoelastic fluids.
method: Train on synthetic oscillatory-shear datasets generated from established viscoelastic models
Use a reduced-dimensional tensor basis to improve physical consistency and numerical stability
Embed the UDE in an open-source finite-volume solver for runtime constitutive evaluation
evidence_signal: The models were evaluated under extrapolation to unseen extensional, 2D, and 3D benchmark flows, including sudden contraction and cross-slot cases, with improved fidelity when first normal stress difference information was included.
watch_item: Performance degrades as extrapolation increases, so deployment to flows far from the training regime may require additional observables or retraining.
paper_title: Harnessing Machine Learning for Hybrid Constitutive Modelling of Viscoelastic Fluid Flows in Computational Rheology
